name: Tests that mock the system under test and assert only on mock calls

planted_issues.description: In setUp, PaymentService itself is mocked (`self.service = MagicMock(spec=PaymentService)`). All tests in TestPaymentProcessing then call methods on the mock, not on a real PaymentService. This means the tests verify that MagicMock works, not that PaymentService works.

Tests assert only that mock methods were called (assert_called_once, self.service.refund.called) but never check the actual return value or behavior. test_process_payment_returns_success stores the result but never asserts on it. test_refund_updates_status asserts .called but not the return value.

test_payments is a class-level mutable list that accumulates data across test runs via setUp. Tests are not isolated — each test appends to the shared list, so the list grows with each test method. If tests depend on list length, they become order-dependent and flaky.

clean_areas.description: TestConnectionHandling.test_connection_timeout properly instantiates a real PaymentGateway and tests actual timeout behavior with a real exception assertion.
